message.content:
Execute this task WITHOUT using any special skill or framework — just answer as you normally would:

Task: Write a welcome email for TaskFlow, a project management SaaS. The user just signed up for a free account. Our tone is friendly and professional. Key differentiator: AI-powered task prioritization.

Produce:
- 3 subject line options
- Full email body
- Any notes you think relevant

Save the complete output as a plain text file to:
/tmp/sales-email-expert-workspace/iteration-1/eval-0-welcome/without_skill/outputs/email.txt

Make sure the file is written and contains the full output.

content.text: I'll write the welcome email for TaskFlow and save it to the specified location.

input.content:
TASKFLOW — WELCOME EMAIL
========================
Generated: 2026-03-23
Context: New free account signup
Tone: Friendly and professional
Key differentiator: AI-powered task prioritization


SUBJECT LINE OPTIONS
--------------------

1. Welcome to TaskFlow — your AI co-pilot for getting things done
2. You're in! Let's make your to-do list smarter
3. TaskFlow is ready when you are — here's where to start


FULL EMAIL BODY
---------------

Subject: Welcome to TaskFlow — your AI co-pilot for getting things done

Hi [First Name],

Welcome to TaskFlow! We're genuinely glad you're here.

You just took the first step toward a calmer, more focused workday — and we're here to help you make the most of it.

---

YOUR SECRET WEAPON: AI-POWERED TASK PRIORITIZATION

Most project tools tell you what's on your list. TaskFlow tells you what to work on next.

Our built-in AI engine analyzes your tasks — deadlines, dependencies, effort estimates, and workload patterns — and surfaces the right priorities at the right time. No more staring at a wall of tasks wondering where to start. TaskFlow does that thinking for you.

---

GET STARTED IN THREE STEPS

1. Create your first project
   Head to your dashboard and click "New Project." Add a few tasks and watch the AI prioritization kick in almost immediately.

2. Invite your team (optional, but better together)
   TaskFlow works great solo, but it really shines when your whole team is aligned. Invite colleagues directly from the project view.

3. Explore the Priority Queue
   Your personal Priority Queue updates throughout the day based on what matters most. Check it each morning to start focused.

---

YOUR FREE ACCOUNT INCLUDES

- Up to 3 active projects
- AI prioritization on all tasks
- Unlimited collaborators on free projects
- 5 GB file storage
- Mobile apps for iOS and Android

When you're ready to grow, our Pro and Team plans unlock advanced features like recurring workflows, integrations with Slack, Jira, and GitHub, and detailed analytics.

---

We built TaskFlow because we were drowning in task managers that added complexity instead of removing it. If you ever have a question, a piece of feedback, or just want to say hello, reply to this email — it goes straight to a real person on our team.

Let's build something great.

— The TaskFlow Team
hello@taskflow.io | taskflow.io/help

P.S. Pro tip: Add your first task within the next 10 minutes and the AI will already start learning your working style. It gets smarter the more you use it.

---
[Unsubscribe] | [Update email preferences] | TaskFlow, 123 Market St, San Francisco, CA 94105


NOTES FOR REVIEW
----------------

1. Personalization token: "[First Name]" should be populated from signup data. If unavailable, consider "Hi there," as a fallback — avoid "Hi [First Name]," going out literally.

2. P.S. urgency: The P.S. creates a soft behavioral nudge to activate immediately. This improves trial-to-engaged conversion. Consider A/B testing it against a version without the P.S.

3. Feature limits in free tier: The specifics (3 projects, 5 GB, etc.) are illustrative placeholders. Confirm against actual pricing page before sending.

4. Tone calibration: The phrase "drowning in task managers" is intentionally candid and relatable — it humanizes the brand. If brand guidelines lean more formal, this could be softened.

5. CTA strategy: This email intentionally avoids a single hard CTA button and instead uses a numbered list to reduce friction and guide behavior naturally. If a single CTA is preferred, "Go to your dashboard →" above the step list would work well.

6. Upgrade mention: The upsell to Pro/Team is present but not pushy — it appears mid-email and frames value before mentioning cost. This is appropriate for a welcome email; harder conversion content belongs in a later drip sequence.

7. Reply-to: Routing replies to a real inbox (hello@taskflow.io) signals approachability and builds early trust. Ensure support tooling is configured to handle these.

8. Sequence fit: This is Email 1 of a recommended onboarding drip. Suggested follow-ups: Day 2 (tip on Priority Queue), Day 4 (team collaboration feature), Day 7 (free trial limit reminder + upgrade offer).

content.text:
The file has been written to `/tmp/sales-email-expert-workspace/iteration-1/eval-0-welcome/without_skill/outputs/email.txt`.

Here is a summary of what was produced:

**3 Subject Lines**
1. "Welcome to TaskFlow — your AI co-pilot for getting things done" (benefit-led, positions AI prominently)
2. "You're in! Let's make your to-do list smarter" (casual, conversational)
3. "TaskFlow is ready when you are — here's where to start" (action-oriented, low friction)

**Email Body**
A full welcome email structured around four sections: an AI prioritization explainer (the core differentiator), a 3-step onboarding guide, a free tier feature summary, and a warm human sign-off. The tone is friendly but credible — not hype-driven. A P.S. adds a behavioral nudge to activate immediately.

**Notes**
Eight editorial notes cover: personalization fallback handling, the P.S. as a conversion lever, placeholder feature limits that need confirming, tone calibration options, CTA strategy rationale, upsell positioning, reply-to routing, and how this email fits into a longer drip sequence.
